title: USDA Value Parser Contract

included: USDA right-hand-side values used by a single text layer
scalar, tuple, array, dictionary, string dictionary, asset, token, path, and value-block spelling
time sample map and spline map spellings as stored authored values
attribute default value validation against declared foundational type names
relationship target ObjectPath listOp value construction
authored listOp field value handoff to usd-listops-authored

model.ParsedValue: A target-native semantic value returned to the spec parser. It may be represented as a raw typed-value variant, typed dictionary entries, value-block sentinel, path handle, asset string, native time-sample map, native spline value, or authored listOp value. It must not be represented as Json, a tagged JSON object, Python dict/list payload standing in for a specialized value, std::map<string, Json>, or a canonical dump tree in domain APIs.
model.DeclaredType: A token-like USD type name used to validate attribute default values when the type is in the foundational catalog.
model.RelationshipTargets: A specialized authored listOp<ObjectPath> value for relationship target fields.
model.AdapterValue: Canonical tagged JSON matching contracts/value.schema.json is an adapter and dump serialization shape only.

output.value: target-native typed value or diagnostic; adapters may serialize this as a canonical tagged value

input.field: metadata field token or extension field name

output.value: target-native typed metadata value or diagnostic; adapters may serialize this as a canonical tagged value
target-native authored listOp<ObjectPath> value or diagnostic; adapters may serialize this as a canonical tagged value

laws: typed value storage must consume usd-foundational-values
path-valued leaves and relationship targets must consume usd-paths
path, token, and semantic name spellings parsed from USDA text must be converted out of boundary strings before returning domain values to the spec parser
authored listOp structure must consume usd-listops-authored
domain parser APIs consumed by usda-spec-parser must not return Json, dict, tagged JSON, or canonical dump trees
dictionary and metadata values must preserve typed entries in target-native value storage rather than map<string, Json> or equivalent JSON object trees
time-sample maps must use numeric time keys internally and FieldValue/value-block entries; string keys are canonical dump output only
spline maps must be native spline records with typed extrapolation, loop, knot, tangent, and interpolation members; JSON/dict forms are adapter output only
canonical tagged JSON may be materialized only by explicit validation adapters, diagnostics serialization, or dump commands
the value parser must not create specs or mutate layer storage
included value production details are governed by contracts/usda-productions/value-syntax.contract.json
unknown attribute type validation remains out of scope unless a golden requires a diagnostic

adapter_protocol.input: Future unit adapters may accept a JSON case with value-parse operations.
adapter_protocol.output: Outputs use canonical tagged values and diagnostics compatible with layer-dump.schema.json.
